name: Haridas Chaudhuri
summary: Haridas Chaudhuri is represented here by a minimal backlog stub so routed on-disk source texts can surface in the Source tab pending fuller review.

engaged_works.title: The Philosophy of Integralism
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored monograph; Chaudhuri's own statement of his 'Integralism' development of Sri Aurobindo's Pūrṇa-Advaita.
engaged_works.summary: Chaudhuri's principal systematic work, in which he develops his 'Integralism' as a reading of Sri Aurobindo's integral non-dualism (Pūrṇa-Advaita): the Absolute as fullness in which Being, creative energy, the natural world, and the eternal ground are inseparable dimensions of one reality. We do not currently hold a usable copy of this text in the corpus; the entry is bibliographic pending sourcing of the primary text.

core_thesis: Haridas Chaudhuri (1913–1975), a Calcutta-trained philosopher and professor at Krishnagar College who corresponded with Sri Aurobindo, was invited to San Francisco in 1951 to teach at the American Academy of Asian Studies; he and his wife Bina founded the Cultural Integration Fellowship, and in 1968 he founded the California Institute of Asian Studies — later the California Institute of Integral Studies (CIIS) — becoming a principal conduit for Integral Yoga in America. His integralism reads Sri Aurobindo as renewing Vedanta through a nondualism that refuses both world-negation and material reduction. The Absolute is fullness: formless Being, creative energy, natural world, supernatural order, and eternal ground are distinguishable but inseparable dimensions of one reality. Individuality therefore has intrinsic value, not as an isolated ego but as a unique center through which Being expresses itself; liberation should deepen into wisdom, love, creative action, and social reconstruction rather than end in withdrawal (Chaudhuri, *The Philosophy of Integralism*, chs. I, VII, XI-XII). Evolution is central to this view. Matter can unfold life, mind, and higher consciousness because these powers are already involved in it, while the superconscient creative force draws evolution toward fuller manifestation. Integral yoga is the practical correlate: inward union with the eternal and outward cooperation with the cosmic purpose of transformation (Chaudhuri, *The Philosophy of Integralism*, chs. IX-X; Chaudhuri, "The Integral Philosophy of Sri Aurobindo," sections on integral nondualism, evolution, and integral yoga).

cross_engagements.brief: Abhinavagupta presses the same manifestation status axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `expressive-manifestation` rather than `real-transformation`.
Alfred North Whitehead presses the same temporal mode axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `process-primary` rather than `both-orthogonal`.
G. W. F. Hegel presses the same evolution register axis in the historical-genealogical register, but with the value `sublative-becoming` rather than `real-cosmological-evolution`.
Judith Butler presses the same social formation axis in the political-social register, but with the value `performative-norm-repetition` rather than `civilizational-structure-shift`.
Baladeva Vidyabhushana presses the same motive force axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `devotion` rather than `bliss-or-delight`.
Adi Sankara presses the same practice-path axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `knowledge-discipline` rather than `transformative-integration`.
Akalanka presses the same soteric end axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `isolation-or-discriminative-release` rather than `transformation-of-life`.
